sections.title: A: Use the word given in capitals to form a word
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap.

items.sentence: Reports suggest that millions of people still live in (1) ...... (POOR), yet those in the richest nations have become far more (2) ...... (WEALTH) over the past century.
Clean water and electricity, which form part of our (3) ...... (DAY) routines, remain unimaginable (4) ...... (LUXURY) for vast numbers of people in the developing world.
Many people question whether it is (5) ...... (ACCEPT) for governments to reduce foreign (6) ...... (ASSIST) when so many nations still struggle with poverty.
Leading (7) ...... (ECONOMY) have argued that greater foreign (8) ...... (INVEST) in emerging markets could help lift millions out of poverty.
Countries with a well-educated workforce tend to become (9) ...... (VALUE) partners in international trade.
The (10) ...... (REAL) is that closing the gap between rich and poor nations will require long-term commitment from governments around the world.
My neighbour came **D** into some money when her aunt died.
items.answer: D – into some money when her aunt died.

items.sentence: Despite her low wages, she manages to put **A** a little money by each month.
items.answer: A – a little money by each month.

items.sentence: Nobody should bank **H** on house prices coming down soon.
items.answer: H – on house prices coming down soon.

items.sentence: It's hard to imagine how they can get **F** by on such a low salary.
items.answer: F – by on such a low salary.

items.sentence: Could you possibly make **B** out the cheque to Peardrop Limited, that would be fine.
items.answer: B – out the cheque to Peardrop Limited, that would be fine.

items.sentence: Every retailer on the high street is giving **E** away free software with every computer.
items.answer: E – away free software with every computer.

items.sentence: He's been saving **C** up to buy a new DVD player.
items.answer: C – up to buy a new DVD player.

items.sentence: I really can't think what we're going to live **G** on now I've lost my job.
items.answer: G – on now I've lost my job.

sections.title: C: Complete the second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
They made me pay a fee to use the hotel gym. **charged**
I ...... using the hotel gym.

Whose credit card is this? **belong**
Does ...... you?

There isn't much flour left in the bag. **amount**
There's only ...... flour left in the bag.

That designer handbag was incredibly expensive to buy! **fortune**
It ...... to buy that designer handbag!

The customer insisted that the manager apologise to him. **apology**
The customer demanded ...... the manager.

Having a car means I don't have to walk to work every day. **saves**
Having a car ...... to walk to work every day.

Don't pay attention to what your critics say; your work is excellent! **notice**
Take ...... what your critics say; your work is excellent!

I don't have enough money to buy that jacket right now. **short**
I ...... money to buy that jacket right now.

Would you be willing to lend me some money? **borrow**
Could ......?
items.answer: I borrow some money from you

items.sentence: The manager promised ...... a full explanation for the delay.
Paul wanted to know why ...... to his farewell party.
Rachel asked her colleague ...... to join them for lunch.
Maria ...... me she had found a great deal at the market.
'What did he say when you stopped him?' 'He said he ...... return the item, but nobody believed him!'
When I spoke to Greg last week, he mentioned that he ...... of changing careers, but he seems to have reconsidered.
'What did your friends say when they found out they were wrong?' 'They apologised ...... me.'
This washing machine is extremely ...... as it uses very little electricity per cycle.
These trainers were half price – they were a real ......!
Do you know what ...... of motorbike that is?
Is there anyone in ...... of the customer service department?
She handed over a twenty-pound note for a five-pound item and forgot to collect her .......
He ...... a great deal of money by investing in property during the boom years.
The shopping trip was exhausting, but at ...... she found a birthday present for her sister.
